mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tight labor market conditions that drive corporate pricing power and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE remains below 4.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining housing starts (HOUST) signals a supply-side imbalance where manufacturing strength outpaces real estate weakness, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the INDPRO-HOUST interaction falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns show negative correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates consumer demand shifting toward durable goods, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle expansion.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS declines and HOUST rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A 12-month increase in CPI inflation above 3% triggers central bank rate hikes, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% for 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction falls below -0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns show positive correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates stagflationary pressures, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines and CPIAUCSL rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during manufacturing downturns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines for 6 consecutive months.
